title: Identify Whether an AGV Fault Matches the Documented Bump Fault Exception Codes
summary: Use the documented AGV Bump fault screen and exception code table to determine whether a reported AGV fault matches the source-defined Bump fault conditions. A match is supported only when the reported exception code is 21057 or 21060 and the displayed exception information aligns with the documented meaning for that code.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing AGV fault information to determine whether the reported fault corresponds to the documented Bump fault category in the manual.
not_for: Do not use to interpret AGV exception codes other than 21057 or 21060.
Do not use to infer additional meanings for codes or descriptions not listed in the source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the AGV fault information, compare the reported exception code and exception information to the documented Bump fault entries, and determine whether the fault matches the documented Bump category.

safety_notes: This runbook is a reference check only and does not authorize recovery actions beyond identifying whether the fault matches the documented Bump fault entries.
Do not infer additional meanings for codes not listed in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Documented exception code table for Bump faults

system_preconditions: A reported AGV fault is available for review with visible exception code and exception information.

visual_references.description: AGV "Bump" Fault Screen used to locate the reported exception code and exception information.

steps.title: Open the AGV fault information
steps.purpose: Locate the reported AGV exception details needed for comparison against the documented Bump fault entries.
steps.instruction: Open the AGV fault information and locate the reported exception code and exception information on the AGV "Bump" fault screen.
steps.expected_result: The reported exception code and exception information are visible for review.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV fault display shows the exception code and exception information fields needed for comparison.
steps.failure_condition: The exception code or exception information cannot be located or read from the available AGV fault information.

actions.label: Locate AGV fault details
actions.exact_action: Open the AGV fault information and locate the reported exception code and exception information.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the AGV "Bump" fault interface and the displayed exception code and exception information.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed exception code or description cannot be located in the AGV fault information.

steps.title: Check the exception code
steps.purpose: Determine whether the reported AGV fault uses one of the documented Bump fault exception codes.
steps.instruction: Check whether the reported exception code is 21057 or 21060.
steps.expected_result: The reported exception code is identified as either matching or not matching one of the documented Bump fault codes.
steps.failure_condition: The exception code is not 21057 or 21060.

actions.label: Verify exception code
actions.exact_action: Check whether the exception code is 21057 or 21060.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look at the exception code shown on the AGV Bump fault screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed exception code does not match 21057 or 21060.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Table 5-1 lists exception code 21057 'Robot front collision bar triggers' and 21060 'The robot triggers the obstacle avoidance.'
AGV BUMP fault exception codes in Table 5-1 include 21057 for robot front collision bar triggers and 21060 for robot obstacle avoidance trigger.

steps.title: Compare the exception information to the documented meanings
steps.purpose: Confirm that the displayed exception information aligns with the documented meaning for the reported code.
steps.instruction: Compare the displayed exception information to the documented meanings: 21057 is "Robot front collision bar triggers" and 21060 is "The robot triggers the obstacle avoidance."
steps.expected_result: The displayed exception information is confirmed as aligned or not aligned with the documented meaning for the reported code.
steps.healthy_condition: The displayed exception information matches the documented meaning for the reported code.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed exception information does not match the documented meaning for the reported code.

actions.label: Compare exception information
actions.exact_action: Compare the displayed exception information to the documented meanings: 21057 is 'Robot front collision bar triggers' and 21060 is 'The robot triggers the obstacle avoidance.'

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look at the exception information shown on the AGV Bump fault screen and compare it to the documented table entries.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed exception description does not match the documented meaning for the reported code.
Escalate if the code and description do not align with the documented Bump fault entries.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Table 5-1 lists exception code 21057 'Robot front collision bar triggers' and 21060 'The robot triggers the obstacle avoidance.'
Exception Code 21057: Robot front collision bar triggers. Exception Code 21060: The robot triggers the obstacle avoidance. Table 5-1: AGV Exception Codes.

steps.title: Record whether the fault matches the documented Bump fault
steps.purpose: Finalize the determination using only the documented code and description pairings from the source.
steps.instruction: Record that the fault matches the documented Bump fault only if the code and description align with the source table.
steps.expected_result: A documented determination is made that the fault either matches or does not match the source-defined Bump fault entries.
steps.healthy_condition: The fault is recorded as a Bump fault only when both the code and the description align with the documented entries.
steps.failure_condition: The fault is recorded as a Bump fault without both the code and description aligning, or the result remains unclear.

actions.label: Record match determination
actions.exact_action: Record that the fault matches the documented Bump fault only if the code and description align with the source table.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed exception code or description does not match the documented Bump fault entries.
Stop and do not infer additional meanings for codes not listed in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Table 5-1 lists exception code 21057 'Robot front collision bar triggers' and 21060 'The robot triggers the obstacle avoidance.'

success_criteria: The reported AGV exception code has been checked against the documented Bump fault codes.
The displayed exception information has been compared to the documented meaning for the reported code.
A determination has been recorded using only the documented source table entries.
healthy_conditions: The exception code is 21057 with exception information matching "Robot front collision bar triggers."
The exception code is 21060 with exception information matching "The robot triggers the obstacle avoidance."
failure_conditions: The exception code is not 21057 or 21060.
The displayed exception information does not align with the documented meaning for the reported code.
The AGV fault information does not provide enough detail to verify both code and description.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed exception code or description does not match the documented Bump fault entries.
Escalate if the exception code or exception information cannot be verified from the available AGV fault information.
Do not infer additional meanings for codes not listed in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use this screen as the visual reference for where AGV Bump fault details appear, including exception code and exception information.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Table 5-1 lists exception code 21057 'Robot front collision bar triggers' and 21060 'The robot triggers the obstacle avoidance.'
